LanternELLCover📄 Printable Pack
EN-ES Printable Resource

My Opposites Book & Activities

A vocabulary pack for K-2 ELL newcomers focusing on common opposite words in English and Spanish, with visual aids and interactive activities.

TopicOpposites
Age BandK-2
Pack Typevocabulary pack
LanguageEN-ES

✨ What's Inside

  • 10 vocabulary items
  • 3 worksheets
  • Mini-book (8 pages)
  • Teacher notes & answer key

📖 How to Use

Introduce vocabulary with flashcards and gestures. Read the mini-book aloud, pointing to pictures. Use the tracing worksheet for handwriting practice and word recognition. The matching worksheet reinforces understanding of opposites. The coloring worksheet provides a fun way to apply vocabulary and follow instructions. Encourage students to create their own sentences using the opposite words.

Mini-Book: My Opposites Book

Print double-sided, fold in half, and staple to create a mini-book.

1
My Opposites Book
Mi Libro de Opuestos
2
Big / Small A big elephant. A small mouse.
Grande / Pequeño Un elefante grande. Un ratón pequeño.
3
Hot / Cold A hot sun. Cold ice cream.
Caliente / Frío Un sol caliente. Un helado frío.
4
Up / Down The balloon goes up. The ball goes down.
Arriba / Abajo El globo sube. La pelota baja.

matching
Name: ____________________

Draw a line from the word on the left to its opposite word on the right.

Dibuja una línea de la palabra de la izquierda a su palabra opuesta en la derecha.

LanternELLWorksheet 3📄 Printable Pack

Worksheet 3

coloring
Name: ____________________

Color the pictures as described.

Colorea los dibujos como se describe.

Color the BIG elephant blue. / Colorea el elefante GRANDE de azul.
Color the SMALL mouse red. / Colorea el ratón PEQUEÑO de rojo.
Color the HOT sun yellow. / Colorea el sol CALIENTE de amarillo.
Color the COLD ice cream white. / Colorea el helado FRÍO de blanco.
Color the HAPPY face green. / Colorea la cara FELIZ de verde.
Color the SAD face purple. / Colorea la cara TRISTE de morado.

LanternELLTeacher Notes📄 Printable Pack

Teacher Notes

🎯 Objective

Students will be able to identify, understand, and use common opposite words (e.g., big/small, hot/cold) in English and Spanish.

💡 Suggested Use

Introduce vocabulary with flashcards and gestures. Read the mini-book aloud, pointing to pictures. Use the tracing worksheet for handwriting practice and word recognition. The matching worksheet reinforces understanding of opposites. The coloring worksheet provides a fun way to apply vocabulary and follow instructions. Encourage students to create their own sentences using the opposite words.

📦 Materials Needed

  • Printed vocabulary cards (optional)
  • Printed mini-books (one per student)
  • Printed worksheets
  • Crayons or colored pencils
  • Scissors (for mini-book assembly)
  • Glue sticks (for mini-book assembly)

🔀 Differentiation Tips

  • **For emerging learners:** Provide extra visual support and gestures. Use realia (real objects) when possible. Focus on 2-3 pairs at a time. Allow students to point instead of speaking.
  • **For developing learners:** Encourage students to repeat words and phrases. Provide sentence frames (e.g., 'The elephant is _____. The mouse is _____.') for oral practice. Partner work for matching activities.
  • **For expanding learners:** Challenge students to use the words in original sentences. Ask 'why' questions (e.g., 'Why is the sun hot?'). Have them draw their own opposite pairs.

LanternELLAnswer Key📄 Printable Pack

Answer Key

matching-1

1.Small
2.Cold
3.Down
4.Night
5.Sad

coloring-1

1.Elephant should be colored blue.
2.Mouse should be colored red.
3.Sun should be colored yellow.
4.Ice cream should be colored white.
5.Happy face should be colored green.
6.Sad face should be colored purple.
